title: task
description: link_layer.rs:336 - build_frame() takes dst_addr parameter and includes it in signature, but hardcodes addr_mode: AddrMode::None. When dst_addr is non-empty, the LLSec byte incorrectly encodes 0 address bytes while dst_addr bytes are still written to wire. Receiver parses dst_addr bytes as payload, causing signature verification to fail. Failure scenario: Any call to build_frame() with non-empty dst_addr produces a frame that will always be rejected by receiver as UnknownSender.
close_reason: Fixed: build_frame() now derives addr_mode from dst_addr.len() via AddrMode::from_addr_len(), returning FrameError::AddrLenMismatch for invalid lengths. The LLSec byte now correctly encodes the address mode (None/Short/Extended) matching the actual dst_addr bytes written to wire.
